claimText: The Uniform Renewable Energy Tariff (URET) was scrapped by the Ministry of Power.
evidenceExcerpt: Uniform Renewable Energy Tariff (URET) scrapped by Ministry of Power.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the current status and the nodal ministry responsible for the URET scheme.
distractorHooks: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
URET is a newly launched active scheme

claimText: The Uniform Renewable Energy Tariff (URET) aimed to provide a uniform tariff to consumers by averaging competitive bid tariffs of similar renewable energy projects within central pools.
evidenceExcerpt: Aims to provide uniform tariff to consumers by averaging competitive bid tariffs of similar renewable energy projects within central pools.
examUse: Concept-based question on the mechanism of URET and how it intended to standardize tariffs.
distractorHooks: Providing direct cash subsidies to consumers
Fixing a single national tariff without averaging bids
Averaging tariffs within state-level pools instead of central pools

claimText: The URET was revoked because procurers were reluctant to sign Power Sale Agreements due to tariff uncertainty over a three-year fixed period, which caused stalled projects.
evidenceExcerpt: Reasons for revocation: Procurers were reluctant to sign Power Sale Agreements due to uncertainty of tariffs over the three-year fixed period causing stalled projects.
examUse: Assertion-Reason question on why the URET framework was unsuccessful and subsequently revoked.
distractorHooks: Lack of renewable energy supply in the central pool
High capital cost of renewable energy technologies
Uncertainty over a ten-year fixed period
